claim: Y Combinator published guidance that instead of selling AI tools, you can charge more by using the tools yourself and selling the finished product/outcome - this is the core model of AI-native agencies
actionable: Shift from selling access to AI tools to selling outcomes - use Claude/GPT yourself with your $200/month subscription to deliver $50k+ of services and capture the margin difference
source_title: Y Combinator Says THIS Will Be Bigger Than SaaS (I Already Built It)

claim: Y Combinator believes vertical AI agents (not SaaS) will see 300+ unicorn companies worth 1B+ each, and this wave is potentially 10x larger than SaaS because you're replacing both software AND the team operating it
actionable: When evaluating AI business opportunities, position yourself in vertical AI agent markets rather than SaaS, targeting the combined software+payroll budget that companies currently spend
source_title: Y Combinator Says THIS Will Be Bigger Than SaaS (I Already Built It)

claim: Don't lead with AI or position yourself as an 'AI automation agency' - instead lead with specific outcomes: 'I generate 20 qualified sales calls per month' or 'I run your content operation' with AI as the invisible delivery mechanism
actionable: In your pitch to clients, never mention AI - instead talk about the specific outcome (leads, content, customers) with a specific metric and timeline
source_title: Y Combinator Says THIS Will Be Bigger Than SaaS (I Already Built It)

claim: For cold email outreach to find your niche, test 3-5 niches with 10-30 emails per day to each over 10-20 days - the niche that responds is determined by actual market feedback, not research or guessing
actionable: Don't overthink niche selection - instead, pick 3-5 possible niches and run 20 days of cold outreach testing to each, then double down on whichever has the highest response rate
source_title: Y Combinator Says THIS Will Be Bigger Than SaaS (I Already Built It)

claim: In cold emails, include a tangible asset in the first message (Loom video walkthrough or custom strategy document) rather than generic 'would you like a call' asks - this dramatically increases response rates
actionable: When cold emailing prospects, include a specific deliverable like a custom strategy doc or Loom video showing what you'd fix rather than asking for a call with no context
source_title: Y Combinator Says THIS Will Be Bigger Than SaaS (I Already Built It)

claim: For cold email infrastructure at scale, you need 50+ sending domains (2 inboxes per domain = 100+ addresses), proper SPF/DKIM/DMARC config, 14+ days of warmup per domain, and only target decision-makers (CEOs, founders, not managers)
actionable: Set up proper cold email infrastructure with multiple verified domains, proper DNS records, and domain warmup periods - and ensure your lead list targets only decision-makers who can approve spending
source_title: Y Combinator Says THIS Will Be Bigger Than SaaS (I Already Built It)

claim: While scaling outbound, build 2-3 Loom-style videos about your work - when prospects Google your name after seeing your cold email, these videos warm them up and increase conversion vs being a total stranger
actionable: Create simple Loom videos showing your thinking and work, publish them where prospects can find them - this builds authority and warms up cold prospects before they meet you
source_title: Y Combinator Says THIS Will Be Bigger Than SaaS (I Already Built It)
